title: Preparation of 100mg/ml kanamycin solution
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fstqm","text":"Kanamycin is an aminoglycoside antibiotic which binds the 30S ribosomal subunit; causes mis-translation. It is used for the selection of cells containing Kanamycin-resistant plasmids on plates and on liquid media at a working concentration of 50-100 µg/mL. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"24rbt","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"44vca","text":"It is most often prepared as a 1000x working stock of 50 -100 mg/mL that is stored at -20 ºC. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"702s8","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"110df","text":"The kanamycin solution is measured and then mixed into a container of liquid or plate media before it has solidified. Cells are then streaked onto the plates or grown in the liquid media. Only cells containing the plasmids resistant to kanamycin will grow.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Clean work surfaces and work space using freshly prepared 10 % (v/v) bleach, followed by 70 % (v/v) alcohol.

Using the electronic balance, carefully weigh out 0.4 g of kanamycin sulphate powder (CAS#: 70560-51-9; Linear Formula: C 18 H 36-37 N 4-5 O 10-11 · H 2 SO 4 ) into an appropriate size weighing boat using a spatula. Kanamycin sulphate is a hazardous substance ( SDS ) and may damage fertility or the unborn child. It is highly recommended to weigh out all antibiotics in a fume hood or powder weighing station.
Carefully tip the powder into a sterile 50 mL centrifuge tube.
Using a 10 mL measuring cylinder, measure out 4 mL of sterile distilled or deionised water.
Pour 2 mL water into the 50 mL centrifuge tube containing the kanamycin powder.
Use the remaining 2 mL to rinse the weighing boat and pouring that into the 15 mL falcon tube to make 4 mL total and mix gently by inversion.
Using the sterile 20 mL syringe, aspirate the solution from the falcon tube
Affix a sterile 0.22 µm micro filter to the end of the syringe and dispense thee solution into a new sterile 15 mL centrifuge tube.
Pipette 1000 µL of the filtered 100 mg/mL Kanamycin solution into 4 x sterile 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tubes.

Close the tubes, label and store them at -20 °C if they are not to be used immediately. This is a 1000x stock of 100 µg/µL Kanamycin which is the normal working concentration in liquid or solid media. The stock can be diluted before use to reach the desired concentration. Kanamycin sulphate does degrade with time and there can be differences in potency and stability between batches. It is recommended when starting with a new batch to titrate the concentration to ensure 100% of non-resistant bacteria are killed. If the antibiotic properties start to drop off, ideally make a new batch from scratch or titrate to an increased effective concentration.
creator: Jennifer Molloy
